prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

Move 1 moves 6H and 5C from column 4 to column 3, which does not reveal any hidden cards. Move 2 draws from the stock, which will reveal the 6D.
decision.reasoning: The highest priority is to reveal hidden cards. Move 0 is the most productive because it reveals the QS in column 2. Once the QS is revealed, the JC (black Jack) currently at the top of column 7 can be moved onto it. This will uncover the 5 hidden cards in column 7, which is the most significant potential gain currently available on the board. Moving cards from column 4 to column 3 (Move 1) provides no immediate reveal and does not enable any other productive moves. Drawing from the stock (Move 2) is a fallback, but the guaranteed reveal of the QS and the subsequent potential to clear column 7 make Move 0 the dominant choice.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

decision.boardAnalysis: The board is currently in a deadlock. To reveal hidden cards, we need specific ranks: the Ace of Diamonds (AD) in column 3 requires a red 8; the Queen of Spades (QS) in column 2 requires a red 9; the hidden cards in column 4 require a red 8 to move the 7C; and the hidden cards in column 5 require a black 8 to move the 7D. While the waste pile contains the 8D (red 8), tableau cards cannot be moved to the waste. To use the 8D, it must first be moved to a black 9 on the tableau, but no black 9s are currently visible. Move 1 (moving 6S+ from column 5 to 2) and Move 2 (moving 6H+ from column 4 to 3) do not reveal any hidden cards, as the 7D and 7C respectively remain at the top of their columns.
decision.reasoning: Since no current tableau moves reveal hidden cards or enable immediate foundation progress, the only viable strategy is to draw from the stock. The goal is to find a black 9 (to place the 8D from the waste) or a red 8/9 to unlock the tableau columns. According to the draw pile information, the next card is the 6D, which is not immediately useful, but continuing to draw is the only way to break the current stalemate and eventually uncover the necessary cards (like the 8H or a black 9).

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

decision.boardAnalysis: The board has several critical hidden cards: the Ace of Diamonds (AD) in Column 3, the Ace of Spades (AS) in Column 6, and several red Jacks (JH, JD) needed to unlock Columns 6 and 7. Column 3 is currently blocked by the 7S, and Column 2 is blocked by the 8S. The waste contains the 6D, and the stock contains the 8H (a red 8), which is essential for moving the 7S in Column 3 to reveal the AD.
decision.reasoning: The primary goal is to reveal the AD in Column 3. To do this, we need a red 8. The 8H is available in the stock. By moving the 6H and 5C from Column 4 to Column 3 (Move 2), we achieve two things: first, we reveal the QH in Column 4, and second, we create a sequence (7S-6H-5C) in Column 3. Once the 8H is drawn from the stock, we can move the entire sequence (7S, 6H, 5C) onto it, immediately exposing the AD. Move 3 also reveals a card, but it reveals the 9C, and there is no red 9 in the stock to help clear Column 2, making Move 2 the superior strategic choice.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

decision.boardAnalysis: The board has significant hidden cards in columns 2 through 7. Column 3 is particularly promising as it hides the Ace of Diamonds (AD), which can be revealed by moving the 7S-6H-5C sequence. Column 2 hides the Queen of Spades (QS). The current legal tableau moves (Move 1 and Move 2) shuffle cards between columns but do not reveal any hidden cards or enable immediate foundation progress. The waste pile contains the King of Diamonds (KD), which cannot be moved as there are no empty tableau columns. The stock contains the 2 of Diamonds (2D), which can be played immediately to the foundation, and the 8 of Hearts (8H), which is the key to unlocking Column 3.
decision.reasoning: The primary goal is to reveal hidden cards and advance the foundations. Since no current tableau moves reveal new cards, drawing from the stock is the most productive action. Drawing will immediately provide the 2D for the foundation and eventually provide the 8H, which will allow the 7S sequence in Column 3 to be moved, thereby revealing the AD. Moving cards in the tableau without revealing new cards (as in Move 1 and 2) is unproductive at this stage.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.
